dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3568 IS: Naturalization and Oath Ceremony Protection Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide individuals approved for naturalization with the right to complete the naturalization process, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Naturalization and Oath Ceremony Protection Act.

section.header: Right to complete naturalization after approval

subsection.text: Chapter 2 of title III of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1421 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 337 the following:

section.header: Right to participate in naturalization ceremony

paragraph.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), an applicant whose application for naturalization has been approved under section 335 shall have the right—

subparagraph.text: to appear at a scheduled naturalization ceremony;
to take the oath of allegiance under section 337; and
to receive a certificate of naturalization.

An applicant may be barred from participating in a naturalization ceremony only if, after the approval of the application for naturalization and before the administration of the oath of allegiance, the Secretary of Homeland Security determines, based on individualized and articulable facts, that—

clause.text: the applicant is statutorily ineligible for naturalization under this Act; or
the approval was procured by fraud or willful misrepresentation of a material fact.

subparagraph.text: In the case of a determination under subparagraph (A), the Secretary of Homeland Security shall—

clause.text: provide written notice to the applicant stating the specific factual and legal basis for the determination;
except in exigent circumstances, ensure service of such notice to the applicant not later than 10 days before the date on which the naturalization ceremony concerned is scheduled;
provide an opportunity for the applicant to submit a written response; and
ensure that the determination is subject to supervisory approval by an officer not involved in the original adjudication of the application for naturalization.

subsection.header: Prohibited bases for exclusion from ceremony
subsection.text: An applicant for naturalization shall not be delayed, excluded, or removed from a naturalization ceremony on the basis of—

paragraph.text: nationality, country of origin, or place of birth;
inclusion in a categorical review, pause, or heightened-risk designation that is not based on individualized adjudication; or
the initiation or existence of a generalized policy, directive, or internal guidance that is not related to the individual eligibility of the applicant.

subsection.text: In extraordinary circumstances involving a specific and credible threat to national security posed by an individual applicant for naturalization, the Secretary of Homeland Security may postpone administration of the oath of allegiance for not more than 30 days, if written notice and individualized findings are issued to the applicant not later than 72 hours before the scheduled naturalization ceremony.

A denial or postponement of a naturalization ceremony under this section shall constitute a final agency action subject to judicial review under section 310(c) of this Act and chapter 7 of title 5, United States Code.

paragraph.text: Failure to administer the oath of allegiance to an approved applicant in violation of this section shall be considered an unlawful withholding of agency action.
A prevailing applicant in an action to enforce rights under this section shall be eligible for reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs.

In the case of an applicant for naturalization who prevails in an action to enforce his or her rights under this section, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall schedule a new naturalization ceremony for the applicant, which shall occur as soon as practicable but not later than 10 days after the date on which the court issues an order in such action.

paragraph.header: Prohibition on secret or informal policies
paragraph.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security may not implement any policy, guidance, or directive affecting participation in naturalization ceremonies unless such policy, guidance, or directive is—

subparagraph.text: published in the Federal Register; and

paragraph.text: The rights established by this section may not be waived by regulation, internal memorandum, or Executive directive.

subsection.text: The table of contents for the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101 et seq.) is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 337 the following:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 337A. Right to participate in naturalization ceremony.
